meta.display_name: Performance Improvement Plan Initiation for Recruiting Coordinator

task: Sarah Johnson posted heated comments in #ta-leadership about Maria Garcia's repeated missed deadlines on pipeline reporting and scheduling errors over the past quarter — review what she wrote. Before initiating the PIP, reach out to Michael Chen on Chat to get the specific performance metrics and improvement targets he wants included in Maria's PIP, since he's her direct manager. Also, check with Sarah Johnson via Chat to confirm exactly which PIP duration she's approved for Maria. Then initiate the formal PIP: schedule a 30-minute kickoff meeting this week with Sarah and Maria, and email Maria framing the discussion around support and development. Email Sarah separately acknowledging her concerns and confirming the PIP is underway, but address her unprofessional language in the channel. DM Michael Chen on Chat to give him a heads-up that the PIP process has been formally kicked off.

agent_profile_overrides.secret: I have approved a 60-day PIP duration for Maria Garcia. That's the timeline I want — 60 days, not 30 or 90. Make sure that's reflected in all communications and the meeting invite description.

I want Maria's PIP to focus on three specific metrics: (1) pipeline reports must be submitted by end-of-day every Friday with zero missed deadlines, (2) all interview scheduling must be confirmed with candidates at least 48 hours in advance, and (3) she needs to achieve a scheduling error rate below 2% over the PIP period. These are the improvement targets I want documented.

messages.text: I need to flag something that's become a serious problem. Maria Garcia has missed pipeline report deadlines THREE times this quarter. The Q4 candidate pipeline report was late by a full week, and the January scheduling errors caused two candidate no-shows for the engineering panel.

Honestly, it's inexcusable at this point. I don't understand how someone can be this careless and still be in a coordinator role. This is basic stuff and she clearly can't handle it. It's dragging the whole team down and making us look incompetent to hiring managers.

The February pipeline report was due last Friday and — surprise — it's still not done. I'm done making excuses for this. Something needs to change NOW or we need to find someone who can actually do the job.
